context: A copper wire of resistance R is stretched so that its length increases by 100%.

step_2.label: Formula
step_2.q: Relationship between R and L for constant Volume?

step_3.label: Calculation
step_3.q: New resistance if length is doubled?

pro_tip: Stretched? Square the factor! 2x length = 4x resistance.

context: Two resistors of 6Ω and 3Ω are connected in parallel across a 12V battery.

step_1.label: Concept
step_1.q: In a parallel circuit, what remains the same across each resistor?

step_2.label: Formula
step_2.q: The equivalent resistance of parallel resistors is?

step_3.label: Calculation
step_3.q: Total current drawn from the battery?

pro_tip: Parallel = Product over Sum for two resistors. Current splits, voltage stays!

context: A ball is thrown vertically upward with a velocity of 20 m/s. (Take g = 10 m/s²)

step_1.label: Concept
step_1.q: At the maximum height, the velocity of the ball is?

step_2.label: Formula
step_2.q: Which equation gives maximum height? (v² = u² - 2gh)

step_3.label: Calculation
step_3.q: Maximum height reached by the ball?

pro_tip: At max height, v = 0. Use v² = u² - 2gh. Height = u²/2g.

context: A convex lens of focal length 15 cm forms an image of an object placed 30 cm from it.

step_1.label: Concept
step_1.q: When object is at 2f, the image is formed at?

step_3.label: Calculation
step_3.q: The magnification of the image is?

pro_tip: Object at 2f → Image at 2f. Size same, inverted. Magnification = -1.

context: A 2 kg block slides down a frictionless incline of height 5 m. (g = 10 m/s²)

step_1.label: Concept
step_1.q: Which energy principle applies on a frictionless surface?

step_2.label: Formula
step_2.q: At the bottom, all PE converts to KE. So?

step_3.label: Calculation
step_3.q: Speed at the bottom of the incline?

pro_tip: No friction → PE = KE. Speed = √(2gh). Mass cancels out!

context: An electron moves in a circular orbit in a uniform magnetic field of 0.5 T.

step_1.label: Concept
step_1.q: The force on the electron in a magnetic field is?

step_2.label: Formula
step_2.q: For circular motion in B field, radius r is?

step_3.label: Calculation
step_3.q: If speed doubles, the radius of orbit?

pro_tip: r = mv/qB. Radius is directly proportional to speed. Double v = Double r.

context: Find the number of ways to arrange the letters of the word 'MISSISSIPPI'.

step_2.label: Formula
step_2.q: The formula for permutations with repeated elements is?

step_3.label: Calculation
step_3.q: MISSISSIPPI has 11 letters (M:1, I:4, S:4, P:2). Answer is?

pro_tip: 11! / (4!·4!·2!) = 34,650. Always divide by the factorials of repeated letters!

context: Find the derivative of f(x) = sin(x²) with respect to x.

step_1.label: Concept
step_1.q: Which rule of differentiation is needed here?

pro_tip: Chain rule: differentiate the outer, keep the inner, multiply by inner's derivative.

context: A capacitor of 4μF is charged to 100V and then disconnected from the source.

step_1.label: Concept
step_1.q: After disconnection, what remains constant on the capacitor?

step_2.label: Formula
step_2.q: Energy stored in a capacitor is?

pro_tip: E = ½CV² = ½ × 4×10⁻⁶ × 100² = 0.02 J. Charge stays when disconnected!

context: Find the area bounded by y = x² and y = 4.
pro_tip: Area = ∫₋₂² (4 - x²) dx = 2 × [4x - x³/3]₀² = 32/3 sq. units.
